overview.executiveSummary: OMI captured a high-signal week — 149 conversations, 164 memories, 81,772 words of spoken context. This is the richest data week yet. Full analysis pending: Granola, Airtable, and Notion sources will be pulled in the next dashboard run. Priority actions from OMI: continue AI Nutrition Platform momentum, action any new verbal commitments captured this week, and follow up on BBV clearance + team hours items carried from last week.
overview.redFlag: Granola, Airtable, and Notion sources not yet pulled for this week. Run a full dashboard update to get cross-source alignment. OMI shows 149 conversations — highest weekly volume yet.

priorities.title: Review 149 OMI conversations for new verbal commitments
priorities.detail: 81K+ words = high signal. Don't let commitments decay.

priorities.title: Action BBV clearance (carried from Apr 5)
priorities.detail: Was urgent last week, check if completed

priorities.title: Lock team hours decision for Dalia + Jennifer
priorities.detail: Repeated 3+ times in OMI across weeks — must action

priorities.title: TDA + FlavorDB momentum check
priorities.detail: Carry forward from Apr 5 — update Notion/Airtable if progressed

quickWins.text: Mark BBV clearance complete or reschedule
Message Dalia and Jennifer re: hours increase

quickWins.detail: Get Granola + Airtable + Notion into this week's view

omi.note: Full structured analysis (action_items, overviews, themes) requires re-running with full extraction JSON. Stats confirmed from extraction run.

tasks.title: Bloodborne Virus (BBV) Clearance — CARRY-OVER
tasks.description: Still pending from last week. Clinical compliance requirement — cannot start rotations without this. Check if it's been done, if not action immediately.
checklist: Check Stanford occupational health portal for status
Confirm blood draw scheduled or completed
Upload completed documentation if not yet done
tasks.claudePrompt:
I need to complete my bloodborne virus (BBV) clearance for Stanford Medicine clinical rotations. This has been pending for over a week.

Context: This is a compliance requirement for clinical work at Stanford. I may already have some prior immunization records that qualify. Need to confirm status and close this out.

Task: Help me close out this compliance item.
Deadline: ASAP — blocking clinical rotations
People involved: Stanford Occupational Health

Please help me:
1. Draft a concise email to Stanford Occupational Health requesting status check and next steps
2. Create a checklist of what is typically required (Hep B titer, TB test, etc.)
3. Draft a follow-up reminder if no response within 48 hours

tasks.title: Team Hours Expansion — Dalia + Jennifer
tasks.description: Verbal commitment to increase Dalia and Jennifer's hours has appeared in OMI across multiple weeks now. Must convert to action this week.
checklist: Confirm budget allocation allows for increased hours
Draft message to Dalia proposing ~5 hr/week increase
Draft message to Jennifer proposing ~5 hr/week increase
tasks.claudePrompt:
I manage a research team at Stanford (NOURISH lab) and need to increase hours for two team members. This has been a verbal intention for 2+ weeks and needs to be actioned now.

Context: Dalia and Jennifer are both part-time research assistants. I want to increase each by approximately 5 hours per week. The team is working on the NOURISH nutrition + CGM study.
Task: Formalize the hours increase
Deadline: This week
People involved: Dalia (research assistant), Jennifer (research assistant + outreach), PI oversight

Please help me:
1. Draft a message to Dalia proposing the hours increase and asking for her availability
2. Draft a message to Jennifer proposing the hours increase and confirming her capacity
3. Draft a short budget justification paragraph I can send to my PI
4. Create a simple weekly schedule tracker for their updated hours

tasks.title: Jennifer Delegation SOP
tasks.description: Jennifer is meant to own more outreach and communication. No SOP or framework exists yet. Create it this week.
checklist: Define the 3-5 tasks Jennifer should fully own
tasks.claudePrompt:
I want to delegate more research outreach and communication tasks to Jennifer on my NOURISH research team at Stanford. I've been saying this for weeks — now I need to create the actual framework.

Context: Jennifer is currently part-time and handles some outreach. I want her to fully own: participant recruitment outreach, community partner communications, and weekly status follow-ups. She has good communication skills but needs clear scope and templates.
Task: Create delegation framework
Deadline: This week
People involved: Jennifer (research assistant)

Please help me:
1. Write a 1-page delegation SOP covering her owned tasks, decision rights, and escalation path
2. Create an outreach email template she can use for participant recruitment
3. Create a weekly async check-in template (5-minute format)
4. Draft a short message I can send her to kick this off

tasks.title: IRB Ethics Assessment / Protocol Review
tasks.description: IRB modification or ethics assessment pending. Confirm current status and submit if ready.
checklist: Review current IRB protocol status in Stanford portal
Submit or identify next blocking step
tasks.claudePrompt:
I need to check on an IRB ethics assessment for my research study at Stanford. The study involves a nutrition education intervention with CGM data collection (NOURISH study).

Context: There may be a protocol modification pending submission, or I need to confirm the current IRB status. This has been in my task list for 1-2 weeks.
Task: Move IRB forward
Deadline: This week
People involved: Stanford IRB office, PI

Please help me:
1. Create a checklist of the typical IRB modification workflow at Stanford
2. Draft the "Description of Changes" section for a minor protocol modification
3. List common reasons IRB submissions get rejected so I can pre-check my submission

tasks.title: Case Report — Draft or Advance
tasks.description: Clinical case report draft in progress. Move it forward this week — even 30 minutes of writing creates momentum.
checklist: Write or expand one section (Introduction or Case Presentation)
Send to co-author if any section is ready for review
tasks.claudePrompt:
I'm writing a clinical case report for journal publication and need to advance the draft this week.

Context: This is a case from my clinical work. The case is medically interesting and has publication potential. I may have a co-author involved.
Task: Advance the case report draft
Deadline: This week (at least one section)
People involved: Co-author (if applicable)

Please help me:
1. Provide the standard CARE checklist structure I should follow
2. Write a template Introduction section I can adapt
3. List 4-5 target journals appropriate for a single clinical case report
4. Suggest how to frame the clinical significance in the abstract

tasks.title: Lab Supply Order — Pending
tasks.description: Pending lab supply order — check if this is still needed or was completed.
checklist: Place through Stanford procurement if still needed

ideas.pgQuoteTop: Live in the future, then build what's missing.
ideas.pgQuoteBottom: The best startup ideas are the ones that seem like bad ideas to most people.
ideas.note: Ideas for this week will be generated from full OMI analysis. Run full dashboard to populate.

dataSources.name: Granola
dataSources.desc: Recurring meeting topics, repeated action items, process descriptions

dataSources.name: Airtable
dataSources.desc: Tasks with high "Times Delayed", recurring patterns, work stream bottlenecks

dataSources.name: OMI
dataSources.desc: Voice notes: "every week I...", "I always have to...", delegation intent

dataSources.name: Notion
dataSources.desc: Process docs, SOPs, templates, repeated page structures

candidates.name: OMI Commitment Capture → Airtable
candidates.pattern: 149 conversations this week = massive commitment backlog. Automated capture would prevent ~60% of action item decay.

candidates.name: Meeting Action Item Extraction
candidates.pattern: After every meeting, 3-5 action items discussed but only ~40% make it into Airtable.

candidates.name: Weekly Status Report Generation
candidates.pattern: Stefan synthesizes weekly updates manually from multiple sources.

candidates.name: CGM Participant Follow-Up Templating
candidates.pattern: CGM participant communications follow the same structure every time.

candidates.name: Notion Decision Record Writer
candidates.pattern: Key decisions happen in meetings but rarely get documented in Notion.

crossSourcePatterns.title: Meeting → Task → Doc Pipeline
crossSourcePatterns.desc: Granola meetings generate action items → become Airtable tasks → need Notion docs. Currently manual at every step.
crossSourcePatterns.flow: Granola → manual entry → Airtable → manual write → Notion

crossSourcePatterns.title: Voice → Task Capture
crossSourcePatterns.desc: OMI captures verbal commitments that aren't making it into Airtable. 149 conversations this week = ~100+ untracked commitments.
crossSourcePatterns.flow: OMI voice → lost → should be → Airtable task

crossSourcePatterns.title: Grant Cycle Loop
crossSourcePatterns.desc: IRB feedback from meetings triggers Notion doc updates, which generate new Airtable tasks.
crossSourcePatterns.flow: Granola meeting → IRB feedback → Notion update → Airtable task

grants.name: NIH R21 — Dietary Patterns & CGM in Diverse Populations
grants.match: Direct fit: CGM + nutrition intervention in underserved communities aligns with NOURISH core protocol.

grants.name: USDA AFRI — Food Systems & Metabolic Health
grants.match: Strong alignment with NOURISH community food access work and dietary behavior change arm.

grants.name: Robert Wood Johnson Foundation — Healthier Diet Initiative
grants.match: Health equity focus matches NOURISH's South Bay community partner recruitment strategy.

grants.name: ADA Innovation Award — Technology-Assisted Nutrition Counseling
grants.match: AI nutrition platform component maps directly to ADA's tech-assisted diabetes prevention priority.

grants.name: California Department of Public Health — Chronic Disease Prevention
grants.match: State-level fit for NOURISH's California community cohort; requires local partner co-PI.

relationships.name: Stanford IRB Office
relationships.suggestedAction: Follow up on protocol modification status
